summary: Iteration 3 regression + Round-4 WebGL/CustomCursor/Magnetic verification for Yachi Darji portfolio. All acceptance items pass. WebGL canvas (data-testid=webgl-layer) mounts on the headless desktop viewport (WebGL2 available), is fixed z-0 with pointer-events:none, and the particle field is clearly visible in screenshots. Root markup restructure with `relative z-10` content wrapper does NOT block interactions: elementFromPoint over hero-cta-work and contact-submit resolves to the actual BUTTON (not the canvas). Hero CTAs work despite Magnetic wrapper (hero-cta-work scrolls to Work, hero-cta-resume href=/yachi-darji-resume.pdf). All nav links present + scrollable; Projects filters + 5 cards render; RentPilot & SupplyChainIQ architecture modals open with 5 project-specific nodes and close cleanly (element removed from DOM). Labelmaster scoreboard/data toggle works, recharts bars render (visible in screenshot). Timeline 2022-2026 buttons present. Terminal opens via terminal-open-btn; help/whoami/projects/'sudo hire yachi' all execute. Command Palette opens via Ctrl+K, palette-input takes filter text, Escape removes it from the DOM. Contact form submits successfully: POST /api/contact -> 200, sonner toast 'message.transmitted' shown. Backend: 6/6 pytest pass (health, contact 200, invalid email 422, missing fields 422, empty message 422, rate limit 429 after 5 valid posts on 127.0.0.1:8001). No page errors and 0 console errors (excluding the benign @mediapipe source-map warning noted in the request).

minor.issue: Rate limit still keys off request.client.host, so behind the k8s ingress load balancer a real client may see up to ~2x the intended budget before 429 (pod IPs alternate). Same note as iteration 2 — not a regression.

action_items: (Optional carry-over) Harden rate limit by reading X-Forwarded-For instead of request.client.host so the bucket keys off the real client IP behind the ingress.
critical_code_review_comments: WebGLBackground correctly uses fixed inset-0 z-0 pointer-events-none on the wrapper, so the DOM tree above it (content is inside relative z-10) receives all pointer events — verified via elementFromPoint at hero-cta-work and contact-submit centers.

Magnetic wrapper is a motion.div with display:inline-block and no pointer-events overrides; onMouseMove only calls x/y.set(), does not stopPropagation. Clicks on the child <button> fire normally. Reduced-motion short-circuits the transform. Good implementation.

CustomCursor mounts only on `(pointer: fine) && !prefers-reduced-motion`, toggles a `custom-cursor-active` html class so the native cursor can be hidden via CSS. Both cursor layers are pointer-events:none z-[100], won't block clicks.

Capability gate (three/capability.js) hard-skips WebGL when webgl2 context is unavailable, or on constrained hardware/touch+small screens. Lazy() + Suspense fallback:null keeps the hero interactive before the Three.js bundle finishes loading — good perf hygiene.

App root markup restructure keeps overlays (Terminal/CommandPalette/Toaster/terminal-open-btn) outside the z-10 content wrapper at higher z-indices (40/60/100). No overlays are being blocked by the canvas.
Contact API + form still 200 with 'message.transmitted' toast; no regression from the wrapper change.
seed_data_creation: TEST_ prefixed contact rows inserted via /api/contact during pytest and UI submission. No cleanup required.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend has in-memory 5/10min rate limit; if you need to retest rate limiting hit http://127.0.0.1:8001 directly (not the ingress) or `sudo supervisorctl restart backend` to reset the bucket. The headless desktop viewport DOES have WebGL2, so the particle canvas mounts (see screenshot). If a future headless env lacks WebGL2, capability gate will skip the canvas entirely — that's the intended graceful fallback and the app must still fully work without it (verified via elementFromPoint checks that don't depend on canvas presence).
rca_of_the_issue: n/a — no bugs. The two Round-4 risks flagged by the main agent (fixed canvas blocking clicks, Magnetic wrapper swallowing clicks) are both empirically ruled out: pointer-events:none on the canvas wrapper + Magnetic being a transparent motion.div that only transforms means all click/keyboard interactions on hero-cta-work and contact-submit dispatch to the underlying button as expected.
